content.problemStatement:
A school has organized a field trip for a class of NNN students, of which XXX students are boys, and the remaining students are girls.

Everyone is excited to go trekking, and must form groups of size exactly KKK to do so. However, boys will only form groups among themselves, and girls will only form groups among themselves.

Both boys and girls will form as many groups as possible.

The remaining students can either dance around a bonfire, or just read books.

Dancing around the bonfire requires a pair of one girl and one boy, while reading is done alone.

Reading is much more boring than dancing, so students will try to avoid it.

What's the minimum number of students who will be engaged in reading?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, XXX and KKK — the total number of students, the number of boys and the number of students in each trekking group.


Output Format

For each test case, output on a new line the  the minimum number of students engaged in reading.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, X and K — total students, number of boys and required group size.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line: the minimum possible number of students who will have to read.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: boys left = 4%3=1, girls left = 8%3=2, diff=1.\nTest case 2: both remainders are 0, diff=0.\nTest case 3: boys left = 8%3=2, girls left = 0%3=0, diff=2.

solution.approach: For each gender we can form floor(count / K) full trekking groups. The remaining students are count % K. Let rB = X % K (boys left) and rG = (N - X) % K (girls left). A dancing pair consumes one boy and one girl, so we can create min(rB, rG) pairs. The rest have to read, which equals (rB - min) + (rG - min) = |rB - rG|. Hence answer = abs((X % K) - ((N - X) % K)). This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); X = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        rB = X % K
        rG = (N - X) % K
        out.append(str(abs(rB - rG)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
